paper_title: Study area and sampling procedures in northern Spitsbergen fjords

methods.description: The procedure for collecting surface sediment samples from various fjords, tidal plains, and lakes in northern Spitsbergen to analyze their chemical composition and environmental characteristics.

steps.description: Sampling stations were situated in fjords, tidal plains and lakes and were labelled as I-III for the three fjords (Wijdefjorden, Bockfjorden and Woodfjorden, respectively), IV-VI for the areas near the respective fjord mouths, TP for tidal plains and L for lakes.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and label sampling stations in fjords, tidal plains, and lakes
inputs: geographical locations of fjords, tidal plains, and lakes

steps.description: Surface sediment samples were collected with an Ekman grab (23 x 23 cm) between 22 and 30 July 2005 at 34 sampling stations located within the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect surface sediment samples using an Ekman grab

steps.description: The water temperature was measured in situ.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure water temperature at sampling sites

steps.description: Simultaneously, description of sampling sites including, among other factors, their morphological and hydrological characteristics was made.
steps.searchable_summary: Document morphological and hydrological characteristics of sampling sites
